title: thelypteris marginalis
intro: Thelypteris marginalis, also known as the marginal-leaved maiden fern, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant is native to the subtropical regions of Asia and is often found growing near streams and rivers. The marginal-leaved maiden fern has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Thelypteris marginalis include reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and soothing skin irritations, with herbal preparations typically involving infusions or decoctions of the plant's leaves.
